title: Lloydâ€™s Register Issues First Guidance Notes for Onboard Hydrogen Generation
content: Lloydâ€™s Register (LR) has published the maritime industryâ€™s first dedicated Guidance Notes for onboard hydrogen generation, providing a critical framework for the safe design and integration of these systems. Responding to growing interest in producing hydrogen onboard using alternative fuels such as LNG, methanol, and ammonia, the guidance offers a solution to the current lack of bunkering infrastructure and the challenges of storing bulky compressed or liquefied hydrogen. By enabling onboard production, the notes help shipowners avoid complex storage systems and facilitate compliance with tightening emission regulations. Drawing on existing rules for fuel cells and low-flashpoint fuels, LRâ€™s risk-based framework addresses safety challenges involving multiple gases, aiming to reduce regulatory uncertainty. This development supports shipowners and yards in accelerating projects, shortening approval timelines, and de-risking investments in cleaner fuel technologies.
author: Analysts Account
